title: polypodium glaucum
intro: Polypodium glaucum, also known as Polypodium leucotomos, is a type of fern native to tropical regions, particularly in Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Polypodium glaucum include protecting the skin from damage caused by UV radiation, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as extracts, capsules, and creams, which are often used topically to support skin health and protect against environmental stressors.
